judgment.summary: Hims & Hers shows strong gross margins and positive free cash flow but faces declining earnings estimates and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (ending 2026-03-31), EPS actual of -0.40 missed the estimate of 0.03 by -1267.9%. Next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Gross margin of 72.9% indicates strong pricing power on its services.
Free cash flow of $174.31M (TTM) shows the business is generating cash.
Total cash of $750.88M provides a cushion for operations.
Price/Sales of 2.19x is reasonable given the gross margins.
negatives: Operating margin of -7.9% and profit margin of -0.6% indicate unprofitability.
Return on equity of -2.7% shows poor returns for shareholders.
Debt/Equity ratio of 253.72x is very high, suggesting significant financial leverage.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (-54.5%) and fiscal year (-15.3%) are negative.
Beta of 2.42 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Hims & Hers Health recently priced an upsized $350 million convertible notes offering, intended to fund international expansion and AI investments. This news follows reports of the CEO selling a significant number of shares and a notable earnings miss, which contributed to a stock price decline. Analysts are also scrutinizing questions from the company's recent Q1 earnings call, suggesting ongoing evaluation of the company's performance and strategy.
